goal: Rebuild the reading.html question-result rendering to match the design doc's ScreenQuickPullResult (1-card big centered) and ScreenSpread (3-card stacked rows with floating position pills) — both layouts driven by a single data-spread-count attribute set by js/reading.js renderQuestion. Add Roman-numeral display for Major Arcana cards, a lucky-color chip on the Quick layout, bilingual position pills on Story, bilingual card name display, and an inline 2-sentence interpretation (EN + TH) per card. Keep all functional panels (topic-story, takeaway, energy, phase, retention, internal-links) and restyle them with --mt-* tokens.
done: js/reading.js: Added two helpers above renderQuestion. getMajorArcanaRoman(card) returns the Roman numeral for Major Arcana (skips Minor Arcana by checking that the card_id does not include '-of-', and clamps numerology_value to 0..21). buildLuckyColorChip(card) builds the design-doc's lucky-color chip (swatch + bilingual label) from card.color_palette[0] using the existing resolveCssColor / isHexColor / hexToName helpers. renderQuestion: sets readingContent.dataset.spreadCount and spreadPanel.dataset.spreadCount to String(orderedCards.length) — '1' for Quick, '3' for Story; CSS keys all layout differences off this attribute. Prepends a dashed .panel--question-recap section showing 'Your question · คำถามของคุณ' eyebrow + topic title (placeholder until the textarea wiring lands). Inside the spread-card forEach, each card now renders: bilingual floating .spread-position-pill (CSS shows only on Story); existing buildCardArt; bilingual .spread-orientation eyebrow with optional Roman numeral appended; preserved .spread-label (CSS-hidden in both Quick + Story now); .spread-card-name (EN serif) + .spread-card-name-th (Thai serif via alias_th); .spread-lucky-chip via buildLuckyColorChip on Quick only; inline 2-sentence interpretation EN + TH paragraphs (.spread-inline-text) using tarot_imply_{en,th} on Quick and standalone_{position}_{en,th} on Story. All existing data injection points preserved: persistReadingHistory, trackRawCompletion, openCardSheet click binding, the topic-story / suggestion+meta / takeaway / energy / phase / retention / internal-links append chain — none touched. css/reading.css: full rewrite, 16 sections, 552 lines, 75 --mt-* token references, zero hex literals. Sections: page bg (--mt-bg-gradient-daily-after), page-shell layout (max-width 440px), .reading-card transparent reset, .reading-intro visually-hidden via :has(.panel--question-recap) (kept in DOM for SEO), .reading-content flex-column gap, .panel--question-recap dashed surface, .panel--spread layout swap (Quick: flex-center single card + aura ::before glow; Story: flex-column rows with gap), .reading-spread-card layout-per-count (Quick = transparent column-centered, Story = rgba-white card with --mt-soft-shadow), .spread-position-pill plum-on-cream chip absolute-positioned -10px top with floating Past/Present/Future bilingual labels, card-art sizing (220px on Quick, 86px on Story), .spread-caption + name + Thai name typography swap per layout, .spread-lucky-chip pill style with swatch + bilingual label, .spread-inline-text EN + TH paragraphs with white surface card on Quick / lighter inline on Story, .panel--question-story restyled with --mt-* surface + serif h3, takeaway / energy / phase / retention / internal-links panels restyled to .panel surface treatment, .action-row buttons restyled as gold-grad primary + plum-bordered ghost pills, .footer-nav-pill stack restyled with hover lift. No hex literals — the existing page bg gradient already had a token (--mt-bg-gradient-daily-after, added in Phase 5 daily for this exact reuse). reading.html static markup NOT touched — all design-doc structure is JS-injected by renderQuestion. node --check js/reading.js passes. cp + cap sync ios; md5 canonical == www == ios for both changed files.
affects: meowtarot.com/reading.html?mode=question&spread=quick — Quick Pull result now renders the design's ceremonial single-big-card layout (220px card art centered with aura glow ::before, large italic-serif card name + Thai serif name, bilingual orientation eyebrow with Roman numeral for Major Arcana like 'Upright · VI', lucky-color chip, 2-sentence inline interpretation EN+TH inside a white-surface card). The dashed question recap panel sits above; existing takeaway / energy / phase / retention / internal-links panels follow below, restyled to feel consistent with the design.

meowtarot.com/reading.html?mode=question&spread=story — Story spread result now renders 3 stacked rows, each with a floating bilingual position pill (Past · อดีต / Present · ปัจจุบัน / Future · อนาคต) anchored -10px above the card, an 86px card art thumbnail on the left, and a flex caption on the right with orientation eyebrow + EN card name + Thai card name + the standalone position-specific 2-sentence interpretation EN+TH. The existing topic-story panel below contributes deeper topic-specific paragraphs (love/career/finance/etc) per position; takeaway + energy + retention follow.

meowtarot.com/reading.html?mode=daily and ?mode=full — renderDaily and renderFull paths are untouched. Daily-mode result and Full-mode (Celtic Cross) result use their own rendering paths that do NOT set data-spread-count. The css/reading.css selectors that key off [data-spread-count] are no-ops on those modes; the .panel surface restyle still applies.
notes: PRESERVED data injection points: persistReadingHistory('question', orderedCards); trackRawCompletion (mode/topic/cards/completionId); openCardSheet(card) click binding on each spread-card; buildCardArt(card, 'thumb'); the topic-story panel with topicConfig.spreadKeys per position; buildSuggestionPanel + buildMetaPanel on the generic/other branch; buildQuestionTakeawayPanel; buildEnergyPanel; appendReadingInternalLinks. PER USER DECISION: extra panels (takeaway, energy, retention, phase, internal-links) are KEPT and restyled, not stripped — honors CLAUDE.md hard rule 'free reading result must always feel complete'. PER USER DECISION: layout swap via data-spread-count CSS attribute, not JS DOM refactor — cleaner cascade, less regression risk. PER USER DECISION: Roman numeral + lucky chip added on Quick. Roman numeral helper uses card_id pattern matching (-of- excludes Minor Arcana) + numerology_value 0..21 + a Roman-numerals dictionary. Lucky chip reuses normalizeColorArray / resolveCssColor / isHexColor / hexToName helpers (lines 574, 676, 689, 716 of reading.js). The .spread-label element is now CSS-hidden on both Quick (no position concept) and Story (position is in the floating pill) — but kept in the DOM for screen readers. The 3 action buttons (#saveBtn / #newReadingBtn / #shareBtn) all stay visible — the design's 2-button-per-screen pattern is approximated by the existing 3 functional buttons, restyled. Future commit could swap labels per spread count if product wants exact design match. NO PUSH per user instruction — local server on port 8000 (started during the homepage push) is serving the new code for review.
verified: md5 canonical == www == ios for js/reading.js (a9d1752284baf4d2f2ab1cbb00d3af85) and css/reading.css
grep classes in deployed reading.js (via local server): getMajorArcanaRoman + buildLuckyColorChip helpers present, panel--question-recap + spread-position-pill class strings present
css/reading.css hex literals: zero (75 --mt-* references; the page bg gradient reuses the existing --mt-bg-gradient-daily-after token that was pre-added in Phase 5 daily)
cap sync ios completed; no plugin errors

reading.html static markup unchanged — design-doc structure all JS-injected by renderQuestion (recap panel + per-card position pill / orientation+roman / bilingual card name / lucky chip / inline interpretation)
filed: Action-row button labels should switch per spread-count to match design exactly (Quick: 'Ask another · ถามอีก' + 'Share · แชร์'; Story: 'Save reading' + 'Share reading · แชร์'). Current 3 buttons (#saveBtn / #newReadingBtn / #shareBtn) all stay visible with their existing labels; restyle only.

When the textarea on question.html is wired (filed in 2026-05-31-phase5-ask-flow-structural-rebuild), update renderQuestion's .panel--question-recap text source from topic title → typed question text (fall back to topic title if empty).

Roman numeral display assumes the card has numerology_value 0..21 and a card_id that does not include '-of-'. If a future Minor Arcana card is mis-tagged (numerology_value 0..21 + no '-of-'), it would incorrectly receive a Roman numeral. Audit card data when adding new decks.
Lucky color chip currently uses color_palette[0]. Some cards may have multiple lucky colors worth showing — design doc only shows one chip but follow-up could render multiple as a chip row.

Daily-mode and Full-mode results on reading.html were intentionally not restyled this round. The .panel surface restyle in §13 of css/reading.css cascades down to them anyway because the same class selectors apply. Verify daily and full-mode result pages render correctly with the new --mt-* surface treatment (no regression expected; this section uses the same Phase 4 pattern that already shipped).
